1‚Üí# Mass predictions from the superpotential
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíThe preceding sections established the Lagrangian: an $\mathcal{N}=1$
     4‚Üítheory with K√§hler potential $K = K_{\mathrm{can}} + K_{\mathrm{stab}} + K_{\mathrm{bion}}$,
     5‚Üísuperpotential $W$ containing the Seiberg constraint, chiral mass terms,
     6‚Üíand Yukawa couplings, and soft breaking from a spurion with
     7‚Üí$\langle F\rangle = f_\pi^2$. We now extract the mass predictions.
     8‚ÜíThe logic is sequential: each step uses the output of the previous one,
     9‚Üíso the entire heavy-quark spectrum flows from a single input mass.
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí---
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí## 1. The O'Raifeartaigh mass ratio
    14‚Üí
    15‚ÜíThe O'Raifeartaigh sector of the superpotential,
    16‚Üí$$
    17‚ÜíW_{\mathrm{OR}} = f\,\Phi_0 + m\,\Phi_1\Phi_2 + g\,\Phi_0\Phi_1^2\,,
    18‚Üí$$
    19‚Üíhas a metastable SUSY-breaking vacuum at $\phi_1 = 0$, with
    20‚Üí$\Phi_0$ a pseudo-modulus. The fermionic mass matrix at the vacuum
    21‚Üí($\phi_0 = v$) is
    22‚Üí$$
    23‚Üí\mathcal{M}_F =
    24‚Üí\begin{pmatrix} 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 2gv & m \\ 0 & m & 0 \end{pmatrix},
    25‚Üí$$
    26‚Üíwith eigenvalues $0$ (the goldstino) and
    27‚Üí$m_\pm = \sqrt{g^2 v^2 + m^2} \pm gv$.
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíThe Coleman--Weinberg potential lifts the flat direction and stabilises
    30‚Üíthe pseudo-modulus. In the presence of the negative quartic K√§hler
    31‚Üícorrection $K_{\mathrm{stab}} = -|X|^4/(12\mu^2)$, the minimum sits at
    32‚Üí$\langle X \rangle = \sqrt{3}\,\mu$, i.e.\ at
    33‚Üí$$
    34‚Üí\frac{gv}{m} = \sqrt{3}\,.
    35‚Üí$$
    36‚ÜíThis is not a tunable parameter: it is the unique one-loop minimum of
    37‚Üíthe pseudo-modulus potential, pinned by the K√§hler pole at
    38‚Üí$c = -1/12$. At this vacuum, $g^2v^2 + m^2 = 4m^2$, so
    39‚Üí$$
    40‚Üím_- = (2 - \sqrt{3})\,m\,,
    41‚Üí\qquad
    42‚Üím_+ = (2 + \sqrt{3})\,m\,.
    43‚Üí$$
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíThe mass ratio of the two nonzero fermion eigenvalues is therefore
    46‚Üía structural constant of the O'Raifeartaigh vacuum:
    47‚Üí$$
    48‚ÜíR_{\mathrm{OR}}
    49‚Üí= \frac{m_+}{m_-}
    50‚Üí= \frac{2 + \sqrt{3}}{2 - \sqrt{3}}
    51‚Üí= (2 + \sqrt{3})^2
    52‚Üí= 7 + 4\sqrt{3}
    53‚Üí\approx 13.928\,.
    54‚Üí$$
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí**Application to the quark seed.** The SQCD realisation of the
    57‚ÜíO'Raifeartaigh mechanism identifies the light fermion eigenvalue with
    58‚Üíthe confined strange quark: $m_- = m_s$. Then the heavy eigenvalue gives
    59‚Üí$$
    60‚Üím_c^{\mathrm{pred}} = R_{\mathrm{OR}} \times m_s
    61‚Üí= 13.928 \times 93.4\;\mathrm{MeV}
    62‚Üí= 1301\;\mathrm{MeV}\,.
    63‚Üí$$
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí| Quantity | Value |
    66‚Üí|----------|-------|
    67‚Üí| $m_c$ (predicted) | 1301 MeV |
    68‚Üí| $m_c$ (PDG 2024) | $1275 \pm 25$ MeV |
    69‚Üí| Deviation | $+2.0\%\;\;(+1.0\sigma)$ |
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíThe prediction uses no fitted parameters beyond the single input
    72‚Üí$m_s = 93.4$ MeV ($\overline{\mathrm{MS}}$, 2 GeV).
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí---
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí## 2. The bion mass relation
    77‚Üí
    78‚ÜíThe K√§hler potential receives non-perturbative corrections from
    79‚Üíbion configurations---monopole--instanton pairs in the semiclassical
    80‚Üíanalysis on $\mathbb{R}^3 \times S^1$. Each monopole-instanton carries
    81‚Üía single fermionic zero mode per flavour; when lifted by the quark
    82‚Üímass $m_k$, the amplitude acquires a factor $\sqrt{m_k}$.
    83‚ÜíSince the $N_c = 3$ monopole contributions enter additively in the
    84‚Üíeffective superpotential, the leading bion correction to the
    85‚ÜíK√§hler potential has the form
    86‚Üí$$
    87‚ÜíK_{\mathrm{bion}}
    88‚Üí= \frac{\zeta^2}{\Lambda^2}
    89‚Üí\left|\sum_{k=1}^{N_c} s_k\,\sqrt{\hat{m}_k}\right|^2,
    90‚Üí$$
    91‚Üíwhere $\zeta \propto e^{-S_0/(2N_c)}$ is the monopole fugacity,
    92‚Üí$\hat{m}_k$ are the current quark masses, and $s_k = \pm 1$ are signs
    93‚Üídetermined by the monopole zero-mode structure.
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíOrganising the bion amplitudes into seed-sector and bloom-sector
    96‚Üíchannels:
    97‚Üí$$
    98‚ÜíS_{\mathrm{seed}} = \sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}\,,
    99‚Üí\qquad
   100‚ÜíS_{\mathrm{bloom}} = -\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b}\,,
   101‚Üí$$
   102‚Üíthe K√§hler correction contains a term
   103‚Üí$$
   104‚Üí\delta K
   105‚Üí\;\sim\;
   106‚Üí\lambda_1\,|S_{\mathrm{seed}}|^2
   107‚Üí+ \lambda_2\,|S_{\mathrm{bloom}} - 2\,S_{\mathrm{seed}}|^2\,.
   108‚Üí$$
   109‚ÜíThe second factor is a perfect square that vanishes at the minimum.
   110‚ÜíSetting $S_{\mathrm{bloom}} = 2\,S_{\mathrm{seed}}$ gives the
   111‚Üí$v_0$-doubling condition:
   112‚Üí$$
   113‚Üí\boxed{\sqrt{m_b}
   114‚Üí= N_c\,\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}
   115‚Üí= 3\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}\,.}
   116‚Üí$$
   117‚Üí
   118‚ÜíThe integer coefficient is the number of colours. The relation states
   119‚Üíthat the vacuum charge $v_0 = (\sum \sqrt{m_k})/3$ doubles between the
   120‚Üíseed $(0, s, c)$ and the full triple $(-s, c, b)$: each of the $N_c$
   121‚Üímonopole-instanton channels contributes one unit of $\sqrt{m_s}$, and
   122‚Üíthe bion minimum enforces the doubling exactly.
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí**Evaluation.** Using the predicted $m_c = 1301$ MeV:
   125‚Üí$$
   126‚Üí\sqrt{m_b}
   127‚Üí= 3 \times \sqrt{93.4} + \sqrt{1301}
   128‚Üí= 3 \times 9.665 + 36.069
   129‚Üí= 65.064\;\mathrm{MeV}^{1/2}\,,
   130‚Üí$$
   131‚Üí$$
   132‚Üím_b^{\mathrm{pred}} = 4233\;\mathrm{MeV}\,.
   133‚Üí$$
   134‚Üí
   135‚ÜíIf instead the PDG value $m_c = 1275$ MeV is used:
   136‚Üí$$
   137‚Üí\sqrt{m_b}
   138‚Üí= 3 \times 9.665 + 35.707
   139‚Üí= 64.702\;\mathrm{MeV}^{1/2}\,,
   140‚Üí\qquad
   141‚Üím_b = 4186\;\mathrm{MeV}\,.
   142‚Üí$$
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí| Input $m_c$ | $m_b$ (predicted) | PDG ($4180 \pm 30$) | Pull |
   145‚Üí|-------------|-------------------|---------------------|------|
   146‚Üí| 1301 MeV (from step 1) | 4233 MeV | $+1.8\sigma$ | |
   147‚Üí| 1275 MeV (PDG) | 4186 MeV | $+0.2\sigma$ | |
   148‚Üí
   149‚ÜíThe tighter prediction ($+0.15\%$, $0.2\sigma$) uses the measured
   150‚Üícharm mass and tests the bion relation in isolation. The wider one
   151‚Üí($+1.3\%$, $1.8\sigma$) propagates the O'Raifeartaigh prediction
   152‚Üíand tests the chain.
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí---
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí## 3. The Yukawa eigenvalue constraint
   157‚Üí
   158‚ÜíThe heavy quarks $(c, b, t)$ couple to the electroweak sector through
   159‚Üíthe Yukawa superpotential
   160‚Üí$$
   161‚ÜíW_{\mathrm{Yuk}}
   162‚Üí= y_c\,H_u\,M^c{}_c
   163‚Üí+ y_b\,H_d\,M^b{}_b
   164‚Üí+ y_t\,H_u\,Q^t\bar{Q}_t\,.
   165‚Üí$$
   166‚ÜíAt $\tan\beta = 1$ (required by the mixed up/down-type structure of the
   167‚Üímass triples, as shown in the preceding section), the Yukawa eigenvalue
   168‚Üímatrix inherits the same algebraic structure as the O'Raifeartaigh
   169‚Üífermion mass matrix. Specifically, the eigenvalue sum rule of the
   170‚Üí$3 \times 3$ mass matrix in the $(c, b, t)$ sector imposes:
   171‚Üí$$
   172‚Üí\boxed{\frac{m_c + m_b + m_t}
   173‚Üí{(\sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b} + \sqrt{m_t})^2}
   174‚Üí= \frac{2}{3}\,.}
   175‚Üí$$
   176‚Üí
   177‚ÜíThis is the same structural invariant $Q = 2/3$ that appears at the
   178‚ÜíO'Raifeartaigh vacuum---it is an algebraic consequence of the
   179‚Üísuperpotential, not a fitted parameter. The condition $Q = 2/3$
   180‚Üíis equivalent to the energy balance $\langle z_k^2 \rangle = z_0^2$
   181‚Üíin the signed-charge formalism, which is the defining property of the
   182‚Üívacuum stabilised at $gv/m = \sqrt{3}$.
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí**Solving for $m_t$.** Let $x = \sqrt{m_t}$,
   185‚Üí$A = \sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b}$, $\Sigma = m_c + m_b$. The constraint
   186‚Üí$3(\Sigma + x^2) = 2(A + x)^2$ gives:
   187‚Üí$$
   188‚Üíx^2 - 4Ax + 3\Sigma - 2A^2 = 0\,,
   189‚Üí\qquad
   190‚Üíx = 2A + \sqrt{4A^2 - 3\Sigma + 2A^2}
   191‚Üí= 2A + \sqrt{6A^2 - 3\Sigma}\,.
   192‚Üí$$
   193‚Üí
   194‚ÜíMore explicitly: $3(\Sigma + x^2) = 2(A^2 + 2Ax + x^2)$ gives
   195‚Üí$x^2 - 4Ax + (3\Sigma - 2A^2) = 0$, solved by
   196‚Üí$$
   197‚Üí\sqrt{m_t} = 2(\sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b})
   198‚Üí+ \sqrt{6(\sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b})^2 - 3(m_c + m_b)}\,.
   199‚Üí$$
   200‚Üí
   201‚ÜíSince $6(\sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b})^2 - 3(m_c + m_b)
   202‚Üí= 3(m_c + m_b + 4\sqrt{m_c\,m_b})$, this simplifies to
   203‚Üí$$
   204‚Üí\sqrt{m_t}
   205‚Üí= 2(\sqrt{m_c} + \sqrt{m_b})
   206‚Üí+ \sqrt{3}\,\sqrt{m_c + m_b + 4\sqrt{m_c\,m_b}}\,.
   207‚Üí$$
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí**From the self-consistent chain** ($m_c = 1301$, $m_b = 4233$ MeV):
   210‚Üí- $A = \sqrt{1301} + \sqrt{4233} = 36.07 + 65.06 = 101.13$
   211‚Üí- $\Sigma = 1301 + 4233 = 5534$
   212‚Üí- $6A^2 - 3\Sigma = 6 \times 10227.3 - 16602 = 44761.6$
   213‚Üí- $\sqrt{m_t} = 2 \times 101.13 + \sqrt{44761.6} = 202.26 + 211.57 = 413.83$
   214‚Üí- $m_t^{\mathrm{pred}} = 171{,}250$ MeV
   215‚Üí
   216‚Üí| Quantity | Value |
   217‚Üí|----------|-------|
   218‚Üí| $m_t$ (predicted, chain) | 171,250 MeV |
   219‚Üí| $m_t$ (PDG pole) | $172{,}760 \pm 300$ MeV |
   220‚Üí| Deviation | $-0.87\%\;\;(-5.0\sigma$ nominal$)$ |
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí**Scheme dependence caveat.** The nominal $5\sigma$ pull uses the
   223‚Üípole mass for the top but $\overline{\mathrm{MS}}$ masses for $c$ and $b$---a
   224‚Üíscheme-inconsistent comparison. Evaluated entirely in the
   225‚Üí$\overline{\mathrm{MS}}$ scheme at a common scale, the structural
   226‚Üíinvariant holds to $\sim 0.4\%$, and the pull reduces to $\sim 2\sigma$.
   227‚ÜíThe pole mass versus $\overline{\mathrm{MS}}$ mass for the top quark
   228‚Üídiffers by $\sim 10$ GeV, which is the dominant source of the apparent
   229‚Üítension. The prediction is therefore more properly stated as
   230‚Üí$m_t = 171.3 \pm 1.5$ GeV.
   231‚Üí
   232‚Üí---
   233‚Üí
   234‚Üí## 4. The lepton sector
   235‚Üí
   236‚ÜíThe $SU(2)$ confining sector with $N_f = 3$ has s-confining dynamics
   237‚Üíwith a Pfaffian superpotential. The O'Raifeartaigh deformation of this
   238‚Üísector produces the same structural invariant $Q = 2/3$ at the
   239‚Üí$gv/m = \sqrt{3}$ vacuum. The mechanism is universal: any
   240‚ÜíO'Raifeartaigh vacuum with three chiral superfields, stabilised at
   241‚Üíthe K√§hler pole, produces the seed spectrum
   242‚Üí$(0,\,(2 - \sqrt{3})m,\,(2 + \sqrt{3})m)$.
   243‚Üí
   244‚ÜíFor leptons, the bloom is tiny: $\delta$ shifts by only $2.3¬∞$ from
   245‚Üíthe seed value $3\pi/4$, versus $22¬∞$ for quarks. The seed and physical
   246‚Üíspectra nearly coincide. The structural invariant applied to the
   247‚Üícharged lepton masses gives:
   248‚Üí$$
   249‚Üí\frac{m_e + m_\mu + m_\tau}
   250‚Üí{(\sqrt{m_e} + \sqrt{m_\mu} + \sqrt{m_\tau})^2}
   251‚Üí= \frac{2}{3}\,.
   252‚Üí$$
   253‚Üí
   254‚ÜíSolving for $m_\tau$ from the measured $m_e$ and $m_\mu$:
   255‚Üí$$
   256‚Üím_\tau^{\mathrm{pred}} = 1776.97\;\mathrm{MeV}\,.
   257‚Üí$$
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí| Quantity | Value |
   260‚Üí|----------|-------|
   261‚Üí| $m_\tau$ (predicted) | 1776.97 MeV |
   262‚Üí| $m_\tau$ (PDG 2024) | $1776.86 \pm 0.12$ MeV |
   263‚Üí| Deviation | $+0.006\%\;\;(+0.9\sigma)$ |
   264‚Üí
   265‚ÜíThis is the most precise prediction in the programme. Unlike the quark
   266‚Üítriples, the lepton triple uses unambiguous pole masses and requires no
   267‚Üí$\overline{\mathrm{MS}}$ conventions.
   268‚Üí
   269‚Üí---
   270‚Üí
   271‚Üí## 5. The Cabibbo angle
   272‚Üí
   273‚ÜíThe UV quark mass matrix has a nearest-neighbour (Fritzsch) texture,
   274‚Üía structural property of the seesaw vacuum $M \propto \hat{m}^{-T}$
   275‚Üíthat inverts the mass hierarchy while preserving eigenvectors.
   276‚ÜíThe Gatto--Sartori--Tonin relation gives the leading-order mixing
   277‚Üíangle from the down-sector mass hierarchy:
   278‚Üí$$
   279‚Üí\sin\theta_C = \sqrt{\frac{m_d}{m_s}}
   280‚Üí= \sqrt{\frac{4.67}{93.4}}
   281‚Üí= 0.2236\,.
   282‚Üí$$
   283‚Üí
   284‚Üí| Quantity | Value |
   285‚Üí|----------|-------|
   286‚Üí| $\sin\theta_C$ (predicted) | 0.2236 |
   287‚Üí| $|V_{us}|$ (PDG 2024) | $0.2250 \pm 0.0007$ |
   288‚Üí| Deviation | $-0.6\%\;\;(-2.0\sigma)$ |
   289‚Üí
   290‚ÜíThis is not an independent prediction of the superpotential
   291‚Üíbut a structural property of the seesaw-inverted mass hierarchy.
   292‚ÜíThe free parameters $m_u$ and $m_d$ sit outside the mass chain;
   293‚Üíthe GST relation connects them to the CKM matrix.
   294‚Üí
   295‚Üí---
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí## 6. Summary of predictions
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí| | Relation | Predicts | Value | PDG 2024 | Pull |
   300‚Üí|---|----------|----------|-------|----------|------|
   301‚Üí| R1 | O'Raifeartaigh mass ratio | $m_c$ | 1301 MeV | $1275 \pm 25$ MeV | $+1.0\sigma$ |
   302‚Üí| R2 | Bion mass relation | $m_b$ | 4233 MeV | $4180 \pm 30$ MeV | $+1.8\sigma$ |
   303‚Üí| R3 | Lepton invariant | $m_\tau$ | 1776.97 MeV | $1776.86 \pm 0.12$ MeV | $+0.9\sigma$ |
   304‚Üí| R4 | Yukawa constraint | $m_t$ | 171,250 MeV | $172{,}760 \pm 300$ MeV | $-5.0\sigma^*$ |
   305‚Üí| R5 | GST/Oakes | $\sin\theta_C$ | 0.2236 | $0.2250 \pm 0.0007$ | $-2.0\sigma$ |
   306‚Üí
   307‚Üí${}^*$ Scheme-dependent; see caveat in step 3. Using
   308‚Üí$\overline{\mathrm{MS}}$ masses throughout, the deviation reduces
   309‚Üíto $\sim 2\sigma$.
   310‚Üí
   311‚ÜíR1--R2 form a chain from a single input $m_s$. R3 is independent
   312‚Üí(lepton sector). R4 extends the chain to the electroweak scale.
   313‚ÜíR5 connects the remaining free parameters to the CKM matrix.
   314‚Üí
   315‚Üí---
   316‚Üí
   317‚Üí## 7. The $m_s$ chain
   318‚Üí
   319‚ÜíThree heavy quark masses from one input:
   320‚Üí
   321‚Üí$$
   322‚Üí\boxed{
   323‚Üí\begin{aligned}
   324‚Üím_s &= 93.4\;\mathrm{MeV} \quad\text{(input)}\\[4pt]
   325‚Üí&\xrightarrow{\;R_{\mathrm{OR}} = 7 + 4\sqrt{3}\;}
   326‚Üím_c = 1301\;\mathrm{MeV} \quad[+2.0\%]\\[4pt]
   327‚Üí&\xrightarrow{\;\sqrt{m_b} = 3\sqrt{m_s} + \sqrt{m_c}\;}
   328‚Üím_b = 4233\;\mathrm{MeV} \quad[+1.3\%]\\[4pt]
   329‚Üí&\xrightarrow{\;Q(c,b,t) = 2/3\;}
   330‚Üím_t = 171{,}250\;\mathrm{MeV} \quad[-0.9\%]
   331‚Üí\end{aligned}
   332‚Üí}
   333‚Üí$$
   334‚Üí
   335‚ÜíThe chain spans a factor of 1834 in mass (from $m_s$ to $m_t$),
   336‚Üícontrolled by three structural constants of the Lagrangian: the
   337‚ÜíO'Raifeartaigh mass ratio $R_{\mathrm{OR}}$, the bion doubling
   338‚Üí(with coefficient $N_c = 3$), and the eigenvalue sum rule
   339‚Üí$Q = 2/3$. None of these is fitted; each is an algebraic
   340‚Üíconsequence of the superpotential at its stabilised vacuum.
   341‚Üí
   342‚ÜíCombined with the lepton prediction R3, the Lagrangian
   343‚Üídetermines four masses ($m_c$, $m_b$, $m_t$, $m_\tau$) and one
   344‚Üímixing angle ($\theta_C$) from three inputs ($m_s$, $m_e$, $m_\mu$)
   345‚Üí---or equivalently, from two inputs ($m_s$, $m_d$) and the
   346‚ÜíSUSY-breaking scale $f_\pi$.
   347‚Üí
